Modulecg.B


Warnings:
Ignoring paths for analysis
  • gain
  • potential
  • hint
  • expert

Code clean check

Detected a slowdown caused by scalar integer instructions (typically used for address computation). By removing them, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 153.25 to 34.00 cycles (4.51x speedup).

Workaround

  • Try to reorganize arrays of structures to structures of arrays
  • Consider to permute loops (see vectorization gain report)

Vectorization

Your function is not vectorized. Only 26% of vector register length is used (average across all SSE/AVX instructions). By fully vectorizing your function, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 153.25 to 33.62 cycles (4.56x speedup).

Details

18% of SSE/AVX instructions are used in vector version (process two or more data elements in vector registers):
  • 23% of SSE/AVX loads are used in vector version.
  • 13% of SSE/AVX stores are used in vector version.
  • 24% of SSE/AVX instructions that are not load, store, addition, subtraction nor multiply instructions are used in vector version.
Since your execution units are vector units, only a fully vectorized function can use their full power.

Workaround

  • Try another compiler or update/tune your current one
  • Make array accesses unit-stride:
    • If your function streams arrays of structures (AoS), try to use structures of arrays instead (SoA)

Execution units bottlenecks

Found no such bottlenecks but see expert reports for more complex bottlenecks.
No data for this section

CALL instructions

Detected function call instructions.

Details

Calling (and then returning from) a function prevents many compiler optimizations (like vectorization), breaks control flow (which reduces pipeline performance) and executes extra instructions to save/restore the registers used inside it, which is very expensive (dozens of cycles). Consider to inline small functions.
  • randlc(double*, double): 1 occurrences►
    • rcm_ordering_from_adjacency(int, std::vector > const&, std::vector > const&, std::vector >&, std::vector >&): 1 occurrences►

                        Complex instructions

                        Detected COMPLEX INSTRUCTIONS.

                        Details

                        These instructions generate more than one micro-operation and only one of them can be decoded during a cycle and the extra micro-operations increase pressure on execution units.
                        • PUSH: 1 occurrences►
                          • SUB: 1 occurrences►
                            • VCVTSI2SD: 1 occurrences►
                              • VPINSRQ: 3 occurrences►
                                • VZEROUPPER: 7 occurrences►

                                  Conversion instructions

                                  Detected expensive conversion instructions.

                                  Details

                                  • CLTQ: 3 occurrences►
                                    • VCVTSI2SD (INT32/64 to FP64, scalar): 1 occurrences►

                                      Workaround

                                      Avoid mixing data with different types. In particular, check if the type of constants is the same as array elements.

                                      The binary function does not contain any FP arithmetical operations. The binary function is loading 676 bytes. The binary function is storing 908 bytes.

                                      Source file and lines
                                      Modulecg.B


                                      Warnings:
                                      Ignoring paths for analysis
                                      • gain

                                      Code clean check

                                      Detected a slowdown caused by scalar integer instructions (typically used for address computation). By removing them, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 153.25 to 34.00 cycles (4.51x speedup).

                                      Workaround

                                      • Try to reorganize arrays of structures to structures of arrays
                                      • Consider to permute loops (see vectorization gain report)

                                      Vectorization

                                      Your function is not vectorized. Only 26% of vector register length is used (average across all SSE/AVX instructions). By fully vectorizing your function, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 153.25 to 33.62 cycles (4.56x speedup).

                                      Details

                                      18% of SSE/AVX instructions are used in vector version (process two or more data elements in vector registers):
                                      • 23% of SSE/AVX loads are used in vector version.
                                      • 13% of SSE/AVX stores are used in vector version.
                                      • 24% of SSE/AVX instructions that are not load, store, addition, subtraction nor multiply instructions are used in vector version.
                                      Since your execution units are vector units, only a fully vectorized function can use their full power.

                                      Workaround

                                      • Try another compiler or update/tune your current one
                                      • Make array accesses unit-stride:
                                        • If your function streams arrays of structures (AoS), try to use structures of arrays instead (SoA)

                                      Execution units bottlenecks

                                      Found no such bottlenecks but see expert reports for more complex bottlenecks.
                                      No data for this section

                                      CALL instructions

                                      Detected function call instructions.

                                      Details

                                      Calling (and then returning from) a function prevents many compiler optimizations (like vectorization), breaks control flow (which reduces pipeline performance) and executes extra instructions to save/restore the registers used inside it, which is very expensive (dozens of cycles). Consider to inline small functions.
                                      • randlc(double*, double): 1 occurrences►
                                        • rcm_ordering_from_adjacency(int, std::vector > const&, std::vector > const&, std::vector >&, std::vector >&): 1 occurrences►

                                            • std::vector >::_M_fill_assign(unsigned long, int const&): 1 occurrences►
                                              • std::vector >::resize(unsigned long): 3 occurrences►

                                                          • unknown: 17 occurrences►

                                                            Complex instructions

                                                            Detected COMPLEX INSTRUCTIONS.

                                                            Details

                                                            These instructions generate more than one micro-operation and only one of them can be decoded during a cycle and the extra micro-operations increase pressure on execution units.

                                                                • VCVTSI2SD: 1 occurrences►

                                                                          Workaround

                                                                          Avoid mixing data with different types. In particular, check if the type of constants is the same as array elements.
